findings.problem: The chapter escalates the national crisis and bases Hermione's return-pressure on the claim that the Manor work was "starting to apply pressure in the right direction," but the mechanism stays too abstract on the page. A cold reader can tell the crisis is worsening and that the archive matters, yet cannot clearly state what specific operation had begun to help, what observable change suggested progress, or why Hermione's physical absence rather than mere archive access is the operative loss. That asks the reader to carry a lot of dramatic weight on reported causality instead of legible process.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:55-67 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:117-123, add one or two concrete operational facts: what task Hermione and Draco were actually doing at the Manor, what paired-presence constraint blocks it, what sign in the field or Mark data suggested improvement, and what remains hypothesis rather than cure. Keep it scene-sized rather than importing a full-series recap.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:55-67 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:117-123 can answer from the text alone what Hermione was trying to do, what constraint required both Hermione and Draco present, what observable improvement had already occurred, and what is still unproven. Fail if the scene still relies mainly on the vague idea that 'something was helping.'

findings.problem: Harry's confrontation scene slides toward composed, therapist-clean dialogue. The exchange advances through a neat ladder of diagnostic questions, mini-speech diagnosis, and a tidy solution beat, which makes Harry sound more like the chapter's thesis-delivery mechanism than the blunt, slightly rough, occasionally inarticulate friend described in the style bible. This is the exact wise-mentor drift the voice rules warn against.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:155-247 without losing Harry's hard truth. Let him make at least one blunt or imperfect attempt before the 'you were scared' insight lands, give Hermione's evasions more leverage against him, and make the turn to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:247 come out of shared history or visible worry rather than a clean Socratic ladder.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:155-247 still lands Harry's core point but no longer reads as calm coaching: Harry should not deliver more than one back-to-back diagnostic summary line, and at least one of his interventions should be messy, colloquial, or partly wrong before Hermione reaches the admission. Fail if the scene still feels like Harry is guiding her to the chapter thesis with polished therapeutic precision.

findings.problem: The chapter's central moral turn lands in the Harry scene, then the coda runs the same recognition again through flashback and abstract paraphrase before finally reaching the actual new constraint. The embodied recollection at the archive is useful, but the chapter keeps re-proving that Hermione was scared and asymmetrical after the point is already legible, which turns aftermath into explanation instead of using the remaining space for a fresher external or tactical shift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:263-279 so the folio memory produces new movement immediately. Keep the strongest concrete reminder at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:269-271 or the abstract moral paraphrase at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273-274, but not both at full weight, and pivot faster to the genuinely new beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:231-247, the final movement reaches a new decision, return condition, or other changed constraint within the next two to four sentences. Fail if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:263-279 still spend multiple sentences restating that Hermione hid the truth out of fear before the chapter gets to what changes because she now knows that.

findings.problem: The chapter leans repeatedly on the same polished antithetical/negative scaffold: 'Nothing ... No ...,' 'X remained X,' 'The bowl ... The memory did not,' 'The archive remained ... The work remained ... None of that changed ...' By the end of the chapter the recurrence reads as autopilot rather than pressure-specific rhythm. It makes grief sound pre-shaped and rhetorical when the material would land harder through more varied sentence architecture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence design in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:7-19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:73-75, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:275-279. Keep the single strongest antithetical reversal, but replace the others with scene-specific image, motion, or syntax so absence is rendered rather than repeatedly announced.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:7-19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:73-75, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:275-279 contain no more than one conspicuously antithetical scaffold built on repeated 'Nothing,' 'No,' 'remained,' or 'did not' contrast, and the remaining beats of loss are carried by concrete scene detail. Fail if multiple paragraphs still announce absence through the same rhetorical flip.

summary: Continuity and tense hold against the provided packs, and the separation premise is emotionally legible on the page. The chapter still falls below pass level because the public-stakes causality is too abstract, Harry's key scene turns therapist-clean, and the back half keeps re-explaining the same moral realization through repeated abstract and antithetical prose instead of cashing it into a fresher end-state sooner.
